message:
feat(freeze): implement size-based sharding for even code distribution

Previously sharding was based on function count, which led to uneven
shard sizes (one shard could be 6MB while others were 1MB). This caused
Zig/LLVM to hang on large shards.

Now sharding uses a target size limit (200KB by default) to ensure
each shard is roughly the same size. This produces ~66 evenly-sized
shards for TSC instead of 6 uneven shards.

Changes:
- Changed funcs_per_shard to bytes_per_shard parameter
- Generate function code first, then distribute by size
- Always include at least one function per shard (for large functions)
- Updated ShardedOutput struct to reflect new parameter
